description: Realistic top-down shoot-em-up player ship, base movement/action set: idle, bank-right, bank-left, fire, hit, explosion. Proportion affects hull/cockpit detail density, not humanoid motion.

states.name: idle
states.action: Level-flight idle loop: a subtle engine-glow flicker while the ship holds level, viewed from directly above. Proportion-appropriate design density for realistic build: high panel-line and greebling detail with realistic proportion hull design.
requirements: Ship faces toward the top of frame (nose away from the viewer) in every frame, viewed from directly above.
Keep the silhouette and palette essentially identical between frames aside from the subtle idle motion.
Do not draw exhaust trails, motion blur, or background scenery.
Proportion-appropriate design density for realistic build: high panel-line and greebling detail with realistic proportion hull design.

states.name: bank-right
states.action: Rightward bank loop: the ship tilts and rolls toward its right wing while holding forward heading, viewed from above. Proportion-appropriate design density for realistic build: high panel-line and greebling detail with realistic proportion hull design.
requirements: Ship faces toward the top of frame while visibly banking to the right, viewed from directly above.
Keep the forward heading constant; show only the bank/roll tilt.
Do not draw exhaust trails, motion blur, or background scenery.
Avoid asymmetric hull markings, insignia, or text so the mirrored row stays plausible.
Proportion-appropriate design density for realistic build: high panel-line and greebling detail with realistic proportion hull design.

states.name: bank-left
states.action: Leftward bank loop: the ship tilts and rolls toward its left wing while holding forward heading, viewed from above. Proportion-appropriate design density for realistic build: high panel-line and greebling detail with realistic proportion hull design.
requirements: Ship faces toward the top of frame while visibly banking to the left, viewed from directly above.
Keep the forward heading constant; show only the bank/roll tilt.
Do not draw exhaust trails, motion blur, or background scenery.
Avoid asymmetric hull markings, insignia, or text so the mirrored row stays plausible.
Proportion-appropriate design density for realistic build: high panel-line and greebling detail with realistic proportion hull design.

states.name: fire
states.action: Weapon fire: a brief attached muzzle flash at the ship's weapon mount, pose held steady. Proportion-appropriate design density for realistic build: high panel-line and greebling detail with realistic proportion hull design.
requirements: Any muzzle flash or attached weapon effect must be opaque, hard-edged, physically overlapping the ship silhouette, and stay inside the same frame slot.
Do not draw detached projectiles, beams, or floating effects separate from the ship.
Proportion-appropriate design density for realistic build: high panel-line and greebling detail with realistic proportion hull design.

states.name: hit
states.action: Impact reaction: a brief attached spark or scorch flash and a small shudder in the ship's pose. Proportion-appropriate design density for realistic build: high panel-line and greebling detail with realistic proportion hull design.
requirements: Any spark or scorch flash must be attached to and overlapping the ship silhouette, staying inside the same frame slot.
Do not draw detached sparks, debris, or floating impact marks.
Proportion-appropriate design density for realistic build: high panel-line and greebling detail with realistic proportion hull design.

states.name: explosion
states.action: Destruction sequence: escalating attached explosion flashes consuming the ship silhouette, ending on a held final burst frame. Proportion-appropriate design density for realistic build: high panel-line and greebling detail with realistic proportion hull design.
requirements: The final frame must be a stable, held explosion-consumed end state, not mid-motion.
Any explosion flash or debris must overlap the ship silhouette and stay inside the same frame slot.
Do not draw detached fireballs, floating debris, or explosion marks outside the silhouette.
Proportion-appropriate design density for realistic build: high panel-line and greebling detail with realistic proportion hull design.
